note: bot_core.py only. Wire runtime/resolved_markets.json into the exit loop so the bot force-closes positions when Polymarket settles a market. This is the integration half of the resolution watcher — do not start until RESOLUTION_WATCHER_BUILD_001 is APPROVED.

context.problem: When a Polymarket market resolves, the YES or NO token price collapses to 0. The _held_bid() call in check_exit() (core/risk.py:177) returns None on an empty book, causing check_exit() to return (False, '') at line 178-179. The position never closes. This is why trades 200, 205, 213, and 216 all required manual intervention. RESOLUTION_WATCHER_BUILD_001 creates the watcher that writes runtime/resolved_markets.json. This task wires that file into the exit loop.
context.fix_location: bot_core.py exit loop, lines 540-567. Immediately before the check_exit() call, check if the market is in the resolved_markets dict. If yes, force-close at the resolution price without needing a valid orderbook.

step1_add_loader.where: Near the top of the file, after the existing imports and module-level globals. Add a helper function and a module-level cache variable.
step1_add_loader.note: Only re-reads the file when mtime changes — safe to call every loop iteration with no I/O cost when nothing has changed. Path and json are already imported in bot_core.py.

step2_wire_exit_loop.where: In the exit loop, lines 540-567. Insert a resolved-market check BEFORE the existing check_exit() call.

notes: close_trade() already accepts a dict with exit_px, pnl_usd, fees_usd — confirm signature in core/db.py before using.
_exit_reason_counts uses defaultdict(int) so 'market_resolved' key is safe without pre-registration.
The 'continue' skips the rest of the loop body for this trade (no cooldown needed — resolved markets can't be re-entered because discovery filters resolved=true markets).
Do NOT set _market_cooldown for market_resolved — the market is dead, cooldown is irrelevant.
json is already imported in bot_core.py. Path is from pathlib, already imported.

steps: Confirm _load_resolved_markets() is defined and referenced in the exit loop
Confirm the resolved-market check block appears BEFORE the check_exit() call
Confirm only bot_core.py was changed (git diff --name-only)
Manual smoke test: write a fake runtime/resolved_markets.json with a non-existent market_id, confirm bot log shows no errors on next loop

do_not: Touch core/risk.py, core/db.py, integration/resolution_watcher.py, or any file other than bot_core.py
Set a cooldown for market_resolved exits
Start this task before RESOLUTION_WATCHER_BUILD_001 is APPROVED
